title: Making Assumptions
note: Protects Section 1 & 3. Listen to context clues, speaker relationships, and environmental noises to assume who is speaking and where.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen for nouns, terms, or vocabulary that reveal the setting (e.g., *balcony, flat, deposit, rent* indicates a real estate context).
  • Analyze the speakers' tone and address styles (e.g., *Professor, doctor, classmate*) to assume their social relationship.
  • Keep track of transaction terms to anticipate what the client needs (e.g., *parking space, storage area*).
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
context clue Noun Phrase petunjuk konteks / situasi Kata atau suara latar yang membantu kita menebak di mana percakapan berlangsung.
low monthly fee Noun Phrase biaya bulanan yang murah Harga sewa per bulan; kata kunci penting dalam soal transaksi keuangan (Listening Part 1).
washing machine Noun mesin cuci Peralatan rumah tangga; sering menjadi detail penentu kenyamanan unit sewa.

items.q: Speaker: 'We have garage parking spaces available for a low monthly fee... and storage areas in the basement.' What is the speaker's job?
items.a: Identify context: rent, parking, basement storage. Infer role: The speaker is an apartment house manager or leasing agent.

title: Understanding Numbers
note: Avoids Common Typos. Distinguish between 'teen' (-teen) and 'ty' (-ty) pronunciations, and follow zip codes/phone numbers.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Pay close attention to syllable stress: numbers ending in *-teen* have stress on the last syllable (e.g., fif-TEEN); numbers ending in *-ty* have stress on the first syllable (e.g., FIF-ty).
  • Listen carefully to double numbers: "double seven" means 77; "triple zero" means 000.
  • Be ready for 'oh' representing zero (0) in phone numbers and codes.
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
stress Noun / Verb penekanan nada suara Penekanan suku kata yang membedakan angka mirip (e.g., 15 vs 50).
double digits Noun Phrase dua angka kembar Penyebutan angka berurutan sama, contoh: *double two* (22).

items.q: Audio: 'Our office number is seven-two-one, double one-two-seven.' Write down the phone number.
items.a: Listen to chunk 1: 'seven-two-one' -> 721. Listen to chunk 2: 'double one' -> 11. Listen to chunk 3: 'two-seven' -> 27. Number: 721-1127.

title: Understanding the Alphabet
note: For Spelling Tasks. Follow spelling sequences closely, noting double letters, homophones, or silent letters.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Identify common spelling sounds: 'A' vs 'E' vs 'I', 'G' vs 'J', 'Y' vs 'U'.
  • Be alert when the speaker spells names (e.g., "K-I-N-G-S-T-O-N") or lists postal codes (e.g., "SW1A").
  • Note if they say "double L" (ll) or "capital B" (B).
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
spelt names Noun Phrase nama yang dieja huruf per huruf Ejaan nama tempat/orang yang harus ditulis tepat sesuai huruf yang diucapkan.
homophone letters Noun Phrase huruf berbunyi mirip Huruf yang rawan tertukar saat didengar (seperti B dan P, M dan N).

items.q: Audio: 'My name is Kingston. James Kingston. That's K-I-N-G-S-T-O-N.' Write down the spelling.
items.a: Listen to ejaan: K - I - N - G - S - T - O - N. Combine: Kingston.

title: Distinguishing Similar Sounds
note: Aids Transcription Accuracy. Practice listening for minimal pairs like 'fifteen'/'fifty' or word-ending consonants.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Practice minimal pairs—words that differ in only one phoneme (sound), such as *bad* vs *bed*, *walk* vs *work*.
  • Look at surrounding sentence context to select the word that makes grammatical sense.
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
minimal pair Noun Phrase pasangan kata mirip Dua kata yang pelafalannya hampir identik namun memiliki satu perbedaan bunyi vital.
consonant sound Noun Phrase bunyi konsonan / mati Bunyi huruf mati di akhir kata yang menentukan bentuk jamak/tunggal (misal: *card* vs *cards*).

items.q: Audio: 'The room is fifty square meters.' Question: 'How big is the room? 15 or 50?'
items.a: Listen to syllable stress: 'FIF-ty' has stress at the start. Compare context: '15' would be 'fif-TEEN'. Answer: 50.

title: Listening for Descriptions
note: For Diagram Labeling. Follow adjectives describing shape, size, location, and materials to match labels.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Before listening, study the map or diagram. Note key landmarks (corners, intersections, entrances).
  • Listen for relative direction words (*across from, next to, behind, opposite, on the left-hand side*).
  • Identify shape words (*circular, rectangular, triangular*) and sizing (*spacious, narrow*).
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
across from Prep Phrase berseberangan dengan Posisi yang berhadapan di seberang jalan atau lorong.
next to Prep Phrase di sebelah / samping Bersebelahan langsung tanpa bangunan perantara.
on the left-hand side Prep Phrase di sebelah kiri Arah relatif dari sudut pandang pembicara.

items.q: Audio: 'On the corner of my street there is a grocery store. Across from that, there is a dry cleaner. Next to the dry cleaner is a clothing store.' Where is the clothing store relative to the grocery store?
items.a: Trace landmark 1: Grocery store is on the corner. Trace landmark 2: Dry cleaner is across from grocery store. Trace clothing store: Next to dry cleaner (so it is also across the street from the grocery store/corner).

title: Listening for Time
note: Avoids Scheduling Errors. Track dates, specific hours, duration, and time-zone indicators (*a.m./p.m.*).
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen for time changes or updates: speakers often correct themselves (e.g., "The bus leaves at 6:45, wait, sorry, it actually leaves at 7:15 now").
  • Note durations: "from 6:45 until 8:15" is a duration of 1.5 hours.
  • Be ready to convert dates (e.g., "the week of August 28" or "Friday, April 6").
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
departure time Noun Phrase jam keberangkatan Waktu keberangkatan transportasi umum; sering disesuaikan atau dikoreksi pembicara.
p.m. / a.m. Abbreviation sore-malam / pagi-siang Penunjuk waktu 12 jam (ante/post meridiem). Penting ditulis lengkap jika ditanyakan.

items.q: Audio: 'The first bus leaves the station at 6:45 a.m., but that is the express bus. The local bus leaves 30 minutes later.' When does the local bus leave?
items.a: Base time: Express bus at 6:45 a.m. Add duration: Local bus is 30 minutes later. Calculate: 6:45 + 30 mins = 7:15 a.m. Answer: 7:15 a.m.

title: Listening for Frequency
note: For Habit-Based Questions. Differentiate adverbs of frequency (*always, twice a week, annually*) from one-off events.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen for how often an activity occurs. Look for words like *every day, bi-weekly, once a year*.
  • Distinguish between ideal frequency and actual frequency (e.g., "I try to exercise every day, but I only make it twice a week").
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
twice a week Adverb Phrase dua kali seminggu Frekuensi rutin kunjungan belanja atau latihan olahraga.
annual / annually Adj / Adverb tahunan / setahun sekali Kejadian sekali dalam setahun (misal: konferensi atau audit).

items.q: Audio: 'I try to visit the library every day to study, but with my busy class schedule, I only get there twice a week.' How often does the speaker visit the library?
items.a: Analyze: Speaker wants to go every day (intent). Extract reality: 'only get there twice a week'. Answer: Twice a week / Two times a week.

title: Listening for Similar Meanings
note: Overcomes Paraphrases. Differentiate synonyms in statements to match the speaker's true intent.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Questions use different words than the audio. Listen for synonyms of the question keywords.
  • For example, if the question asks for "free", listen for "no extra charge" or "included in the rent".
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
no extra charge Noun Phrase tanpa biaya tambahan / gratis Sinonim dari *free* atau *complimentary*.
included in the rent Verb Phrase sudah termasuk uang sewa Fasilitas gratis yang tidak memerlukan biaya bulanan tambahan.

items.q: Question: 'Is the exercise room free for members?' Audio: 'Is it included in the rent? No, it’s available for a small extra fee.' Answer the question.
items.a: Listen to terms: 'small extra fee'. Compare synonym: 'free' would mean no fee. Answer: No (there is an extra fee).

title: Listening for Emotions
note: For Attitude/Opinion Questions. Listen to tone of voice, sighs, pauses, or pitch to determine how the speaker feels.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen to the speaker's tone: flat tones suggest boredom/indifference, rising pitches suggest excitement, while hesitations (*uh, well, actually*) indicate worry or change of mind.
  • Identify emotion adjectives used (*irritated, enthusiastic, nervous, relieved*).
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
irritated Adjective jengkel / terganggu Kondisi emosi kesal. Pembicara mungkin terdengar menghela nafas panjang.
nervous Adjective gugup / cemas Rasa cemas sebelum tes atau presentasi; pelafalan mungkin agak terbata-bata.

items.q: Audio: (Sighs) 'Even though I was nervous when I first walked into the examination room, the examiner put me at ease.' How did the speaker feel initially?
items.a: Listen to initial emotion: 'nervous'. Identify change: 'put me at ease' (relieved). Initial feeling: Nervous / Anxious.

title: Listening for an Explanation
note: For Flowcharts & Processes. Follow links of cause-and-effect or steps in a process layout.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen for process markers (*first step, then, after that, finally*).
  • Understand cause-and-effect links: words like *because, since, as a result of, therefore* explain why things happen.
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
as a result of Prep Phrase sebagai akibat dari / karena Penanda sebab-akibat. Menghubungkan kejadian awal dengan konsekuensinya.
first step Noun Phrase langkah pertama / awal Memulai instruksi atau alur kerja dalam proses.

items.q: Audio: 'As a result of this increase in blood flow, the system is unable to function effectively.' What is the cause of the system failure?
items.a: Identify connector: 'As a result of...'. Extract the cause following the connector: 'increase in blood flow'. Answer: Increase in blood flow / Blood flow increase.

title: Listening for Classifications
note: For Table-Completion Questions. Listen to details that categorize concepts into distinct groups.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Track categories during the lecture (e.g., "Group A vs Group B", "On-campus vs Off-campus housing").
  • Use columns in tables to anticipate which category is being discussed.
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
categorize Verb mengkategorikan / mengelompokkan Memilah informasi ke dalam kelompok yang berbeda berdasarkan ciri-cirinya.
best suited for Adj Phrase paling cocok untuk Kecocokan suatu kategori untuk kelompok tertentu (misal: *apartments are best suited for couples*).

items.q: Audio: 'Dormitories are for full-time students. Apartments, on the other hand, are best suited for married couples.' Classify apartments.
items.a: Categorize dorms: Full-time students. Categorize apartments: Married couples. Classification of apartments: Best suited for married couples.

title: Listening for Comparisons and Contrasts
note: Aids Multiple-Choice. Distinguish comparative structures (*whereas, like, unlike, different from*).
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen for contrasting words: *but, however, on the other hand, whereas, although*.
  • Identify comparative adjectives (*smaller, faster, more expensive*).
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
whereas Conjunction sedangkan / sementara itu Kata hubung kontras untuk membandingkan dua fakta yang berbeda secara langsung.
unlike Preposition tidak seperti / berbeda dengan Menunjukkan perbedaan kontras di awal kalimat (e.g., *Unlike dorms, apartments have garages*).

items.q: Audio: 'The university campus has many older brick buildings, whereas the new library is a modern concrete structure.' Compare the campus and library.
items.a: Identify campus buildings: Older, brick. Identify library: Modern, concrete. Contrast: Campus buildings are old/brick; library is new/concrete.

title: Listening for Negative Meaning
note: For T/F/NG or MC. Listen for negation prefixes (*un-, in-, dis-*) or restrictive adverbs (*hardly, except*).
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Negation is not always just "not". It can be prefixes like *disagree, misunderstand, irregular*.
  • Restrictive adverbs (*hardly ever, barely, except for*) mean the condition is negative or very limited.
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
except for Prep Phrase kecuali / selain untuk Menunjukkan pengecualian tunggal dalam suatu aturan umum.
misunderstand Verb salah paham / keliru Bentuk negasi kognitif; salah mengerti maksud informasi.

items.q: Audio: 'All students are allowed to park in the garage, except for freshmen.' Can freshmen park in the garage?
items.a: Listen to restriction: 'except for freshmen'. Apply rule: Freshmen are excluded. Answer: No (freshmen are not allowed).

title: Listening for Chronology
note: Follows Timelines. Track time sequence markers (*initially, subsequently, finally*) to order historic/academic lists.
details:
Strategy & Method:
  • Listen for chronological transitions to avoid mixing up the order of historical events.
  • Use numbers or letters on your scratchpad to order steps quickly.
Key Vocabulary & Patterns:
English Pattern Type / Role Arti (Indonesian) Usage Context & Explanation
initially Adverb pada awalnya / mulanya Menunjukkan keadaan awal sebelum adanya perubahan/perkembangan baru.
subsequently Adverb selanjutnya / setelah itu Menunjukkan peristiwa berikutnya dalam alur kronologis.

items.q: Audio: 'Initially, the land was used for farming. Subsequently, a local businessman bought it and built an airport.' Order the uses.
items.a: Initial use: Farming. Subsequent use: Airport. Chronological order: 1. Farming -> 2. Airport.
